No Poverty.reason: No explicit or strong inferred evidence in the course syllabus links to poverty eradication.

Zero Hunger.reason: No explicit or strong inferred evidence in the course syllabus links to hunger or food security.

Good Health and Well-being.reason: No explicit or strong inferred evidence in the course syllabus links to health or well-being.

Quality Education.reason: The course is tertiary education providing advanced professional knowledge and skills for employment, directly aligning with Targets 4.3 and 4.4. It also touches on sustainability (4.7).
evidence: 習得科管與智財相關進階專業知識 (Students will acquire advanced professional knowledge in Technology, Innovation and Intellectual Property Management)
培養面向未來的決策能力

Gender Equality.reason: No explicit or strong inferred evidence in the course syllabus links to gender equality.

Clean Water and Sanitation.reason: No explicit or strong inferred evidence in the course syllabus links to water or sanitation.

Affordable and Clean Energy.reason: The course discusses 'electric vehicles and the rise of power semiconductors,' which are crucial for improving energy efficiency (Target 7.3) in transportation.
evidence: 電動車與功率半導體的崛起 (electric vehicles and the rise of power semiconductors)

Decent Work and Economic Growth.reason: The course focuses on the semiconductor industry as a core driver of economic growth, emphasizing technological upgrading, innovation, and value creation, directly aligning with Target 8.2.
evidence: 科技產業發展的核心驅動力 (core driving force of technology industry development)
產業鏈價值創造 (value creation in the industrial chain)
技術創新 (technological innovation)

Industry, Innovation and Infrastructure.reason: The course's core focus on the semiconductor industry, technological innovation, industrial value chains, and advanced technologies directly aligns with and drives Targets 9.2, 9.4, and 9.5.
evidence: 科技產業概論 (Emerging Technologies and Industries)
半導體產業為主軸 (focuses on the semiconductor industry)
技術創新 (technological innovation)

Reduced Inequalities.reason: The course's discussion of geopolitics and international competition in the semiconductor industry has a weak, inferred link to inequalities between nations (Target 10.6).
evidence: 地緣政治與全球市場的視角 (perspective of geopolitics and the global market)
國際競爭與市場壟斷 (International competition and market monopoly)

Sustainable Cities and Communities.reason: The course mentions 'electric vehicles,' which contribute to sustainable transport systems in cities (Target 11.2). This is a minor, inferred connection.
evidence: 電動車與功率半導體的崛起 (electric vehicles and the rise of power semiconductors)

Responsible Consumption and Production.reason: The course explicitly aims for students to 'understand and identify social responsibility and sustainability issues,' and discusses 'technology life cycle,' linking to sustainable production patterns (Target 12.2).
evidence: 理解與辨別社會責任與永續議題 (Students will understand and be able to identify social responsibility and sustainability issues)
技術生命周期 (technology life cycle)

Climate Action.reason: The discussion of 'electric vehicles' provides an inferred, indirect link to climate change mitigation by reducing carbon emissions (Target 13.2), though climate action is not a primary course focus.
evidence: 電動車與功率半導體的崛起 (electric vehicles and the rise of power semiconductors)

Life Below Water.reason: No explicit or strong inferred evidence in the course syllabus links to marine ecosystems or life below water.

Life on Land.reason: No explicit or strong inferred evidence in the course syllabus links to terrestrial ecosystems or biodiversity.

Peace, Justice and Strong Institutions.reason: The course's module on geopolitics, government policies, and international competition directly relates to the development of effective, accountable institutions and global governance (Targets 16.6, 16.8).
evidence: 全球半導體產業的地緣政治背景 (The geopolitical background of the global semiconductor industry)
政府政策對半導體產業的影響 (The impact of government policies on the semiconductor industry)

Partnerships for the Goals.reason: The course's analysis of the global semiconductor industry chain, supply chain risks, and international competition directly relates to enhancing global partnerships for technology, trade, and cooperation (Targets 17.6, 17.10).
evidence: 半導體產業鏈的分工與價值創造模式 (Division of labor and value creation model of the semiconductor industry chain)
半導體供應鏈的風險與機會 (Risks and opportunities of the semiconductor supply chain)
國際競爭 (international competition)
